af_score_components.notes: MCP server connecting AI agents to SearXNG metasearch engine (quality 75). Optional HTTP basic auth via AUTH_USERNAME/AUTH_PASSWORD env vars. Proxy support available. Result quality depends entirely on SearXNG instance configuration and enabled upstream engines. Upstream engines (Google, Bing) may rate-limit the SearXNG instance — intermittent failures possible. URL content extraction may fail on JavaScript-heavy SPAs. Caching TTL may return stale content for frequently updated pages. Offset pagination. Idempotency: yes (read-only searches). Self-hosted SearXNG required. Good for privacy-preserving AI agent web search without sending queries to Google/Bing directly.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS if SearXNG configured with TLS — varies by deployment. Optional HTTP basic auth. Anonymous access by default — acceptable for self-hosted internal use. No scope control — search queries are inherently read-only. Community maintained MCP layer; SearXNG upstream is well-maintained open source. Auth credentials via env vars. SearXNG hides the actual upstream search engines from the MCP server — good privacy design. No compliance certifications. URL content extraction sends HTTP requests — review outbound access policies.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted SearXNG required — no SLA. SearXNG upstream is well-maintained. No formal breaking change policy for MCP server. Idempotency: yes (search queries are read-only). Upstream engine rate limits cause intermittent failures — design agents to handle search failures gracefully. URL content extraction reliability varies by target site. Offset pagination for multi-page results. Result freshness depends on SearXNG configuration and upstream engine caching. No retry guidance.

af_score_components.notes: 36 Docker-based MCP servers with 175+ offensive security tools for authorized pentesting (quality 65). No auth for local tools; OSINT tools (Shodan, VirusTotal, OTX) require API keys per-container. LEGAL LIABILITY: AI agents can autonomously run offensive tools like Nmap, Nuclei, SQLMap without explicit scope enforcement — creating legal exposure for the operator. No built-in target allowlisting. Building all 36 images requires 20+ GB disk space. Long-running tools exceed MCP client timeouts. Commercial tools (IDA Pro, Burp Suite Pro) require separate licensing. Docker container isolation is process-level, not network-level — containers can reach host network. Excellent for authorized security professionals needing AI-assisted penetration testing.

security_score_components.security_notes: No authentication on MCP servers themselves. OSINT API keys (Shodan, VirusTotal) in Docker environment variables per-container. No target allowlisting or scope enforcement — entire responsibility on operator and prompts. Docker container isolation is process-level only — containers can reach host network without additional firewall rules. OSINT API keys in Docker env vars are accessible to any container process. FuzzingLabs maintained — security tool specialists with good practices. 175+ offensive tools create massive attack surface if unauthorized access occurs. Only deploy on air-gapped or isolated networks.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted Docker Compose — no SLA. 36 containers with many external tool dependencies. FuzzingLabs maintained — active security community. No formal breaking change policy. Long-running tools (full Nmap service scan, fuzzing) exceed MCP client timeouts — design workflows around this. No completion detection for blocking tools. Partial idempotency — read ops (Nmap scan) idempotent, exploit operations are not. OSINT API keys must be configured per-container — easy to miss in initial setup. Docker build time significant (20+ GB on first setup).

af_score_components.notes: Community MCP server for Azure DevOps (quality 78). Three auth options: PAT (simplest), Azure Identity DefaultAzureCredential (managed identity/service principal), Azure CLI session reuse. Supports both ADO Services (cloud) and on-premises Server. PAT tokens have configurable expiration — need rotation strategy for long-running deployments. ADO REST API rate limits (varies by tier) can throttle agents. On-premises ADO Server may have different API versions than cloud. Work item creation is not idempotent — check before creating to avoid duplicates. Azure Identity requires correct AAD role assignments. ISO27001/SOC2/GDPR via Azure DevOps. Good enterprise DevOps automation tool.

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure DevOps enforces HTTPS. ISO27001/SOC2/GDPR compliance via Azure platform. PAT tokens with configurable scopes (work items, repos, pipelines) enable least-privilege. Azure Identity DefaultAzureCredential with AAD role assignments is enterprise-grade. Azure managed identity avoids static credential management. PAT via environment variable. Azure CLI session reuse inherits logged-in user permissions — review for production agent use. On-premises ADO has different security posture from cloud services. Work item and PR creation have team collaboration implications — scope PAT to minimum required permissions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Azure DevOps cloud has SLA; on-premises reliability depends on infrastructure. ADO REST API is stable with versioning. Community MCP server may lag ADO API updates. No retry guidance documented. PAT expiration requires proactive rotation strategy. Work item creation not idempotent — agents must check for existing items. ADO rate limits can throttle rapid sequential API calls. Offset pagination for work item queries. On-premises vs cloud API version differences may cause compatibility issues.

af_score_components.notes: Official AntV team MCP server for chart generation (quality 78). No auth required. Supports 26+ chart types: statistical, relational, geographic, process. IMPORTANT: Default config may send data to AntV cloud rendering service — use VIS_REQUEST_SERVER env var to point to a private instance for sensitive data. Geographic tools (district, path, pin maps) limited to China via AMap. 26 tool definitions may consume significant MCP tool-listing context — use DISABLED_TOOLS to reduce scope. Full idempotency (same data + chart type = same image). Output is static image URLs or base64 — cannot interact further. Alibaba AntV team maintained. Good for AI documentation and data analysis workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: AntV cloud rendering service uses HTTPS. No credentials required or stored. CRITICAL: Default config may send chart data to AntV cloud service (Alibaba) — sensitive data must use private VIS_REQUEST_SERVER deployment. DISABLED_TOOLS env var provides coarse tool scope control. No compliance certifications. Optional SERVICE_ID for personal tracking on AntV service. Alibaba (AntV) maintained — active open source team. Chart data sent to cloud service may include business-sensitive data visualizations. Private instance deployment resolves this concern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: AntV cloud rendering service has unknown SLA. Private instance deployment provides more control. Alibaba AntV team actively maintained. No formal breaking change policy for MCP server. Full idempotency — chart generation is deterministic. 26 tools may cause MCP context overhead in large tool sets. Mermaid-incompatible diagram types require AntV-specific data formats. No retry guidance. Geographic tools limited to China (AMap data). Static image output limits downstream interactivity.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for querying and summarizing chat messages from SQLite (quality 55). No external auth — CHAT_DB_PATH env var points to local SQLite database. Tightly coupled to chatmcp companion chatbot schema — not usable with other chat data sources. Single tool exposed (query_chat_messages) — very limited surface area. Misconfigured CHAT_DB_PATH silently fails. Requires companion chatmcp chatbot to be running and populating the database. Good idempotency (read-only queries). Very niche use case — only suitable for chatmcp ecosystem users wanting LLM chat history analysis.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local SQLite access — no network exposure. SQLite file access controlled by OS filesystem permissions. No external credentials stored. CHAT_DB_PATH env var controls data access. Community maintained TypeScript project — chatmcp ecosystem. Chat history data may contain sensitive personal conversations. File permissions on SQLite database are the primary security control. No compliance certifications. Single tool limits blast radius. Local access controls provide implicit security boundary.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Requires companion chatmcp chatbot running and populating database. Tightly coupled to chatmcp database schema — any schema change in companion app breaks this MCP server. No formal breaking change policy. Idempotency: yes (read-only queries). CHAT_DB_PATH misconfiguration silently fails — no clear error message. Single tool design limits failure modes. Community maintained with unknown update cadence.

af_score_components.notes: DEPRECATED: Official Elastic MCP server for Elasticsearch clusters (quality 70). Use Elastic 9.2+ native MCP endpoint for new projects. API key or basic auth (ES_USERNAME/ES_PASSWORD) via environment variables. ES_SSL_SKIP_VERIFY=true flag bypasses TLS verification — security risk in production. Streamable-HTTP/SSE modes require additional mcp-proxy installation. Read-only operations only — no indexing or data modification. ES|QL and Query DSL search. Docker image available from docker.elastic.co. Clear deprecation notice with migration path. Still useful for Elasticsearch 8.x users not yet on 9.2+.

security_score_components.security_notes: Elasticsearch enforces TLS in cloud deployments. ES_SSL_SKIP_VERIFY=true is a security risk — never use in production. API key or basic auth via environment variables. Elasticsearch role-based index permissions can scope API key access. Official Elastic project (Rust-based Docker image from docker.elastic.co) — good security review. No compliance certifications for MCP server layer itself. Read-only operations reduce blast radius. Deprecated status means security patches may slow. API key preferred over basic auth for agents.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: DEPRECATED — no new feature development. Elastic 9.2+ has native MCP endpoint. Elasticsearch cluster SLA depends on deployment (Elastic Cloud has SLA). Clear deprecation with migration path to native endpoint. Read-only operations are idempotent. No retry guidance. SSE/HTTP transport modes require mcp-proxy for stdio bridging. SSL verification bypass is an operational gotcha. Suitable for Elastic 8.x users only — migrate when upgrading to 9.2+.

af_score_components.notes: Official Qdrant MCP server for semantic memory and vector search (quality 80). Optional QDRANT_API_KEY env var for Qdrant Cloud or secured self-hosted instances — no auth required for local. Cannot specify both QDRANT_URL and QDRANT_LOCAL_PATH simultaneously. Default embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) downloaded at first run — initial startup slow. qdrant-store does NOT deduplicate — repeated writes create duplicate vectors. COLLECTION_NAME must be set or passed per-call; omitting both causes error. Excellent for persistent semantic memory across AI agent sessions. Official Qdrant project with active development.

security_score_components.security_notes: Qdrant Cloud enforces HTTPS. Local deployment optional TLS. API key for Qdrant Cloud provides collection-level access. No per-operation scope granularity on the MCP layer. Official Qdrant project — rigorous security review. QDRANT_API_KEY via environment variable. FastEmbed downloads models at first run — verify model sources. No compliance certifications. Agent-stored memories may contain sensitive context — access control critical for shared deployments. No anonymous access in production cloud mode.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Qdrant Cloud has SLA. Local Qdrant is stable. Official Qdrant project with active development. Good versioning and migration guides. No retry guidance. Partial idempotency — find operations idempotent, store creates duplicates on repeated writes. Initial model download on first run creates startup latency. COLLECTION_NAME must be configured to avoid errors. QDRANT_URL and QDRANT_LOCAL_PATH are mutually exclusive — runtime error if both set. FastEmbed dependencies add startup time for first use.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server providing structured EARS-format spec workflow via MCP prompts (not tools) (quality 60). No auth required. Exposes MCP prompts — requires MCP client that supports prompt invocation. Three-stage workflow: generate-requirements (EARS format) → generate-design → generate-code. Stage 2 reads specs/requirements.md — must run in order. Output files overwritten on each run with no versioning or backup. Node.js 20+ required. Good idempotency. Excellent for teams wanting requirements-first AI development discipline. Not suitable for rapid prototyping or teams without spec-writing culture.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local operation — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. File system access limited to project directory for spec artifact generation. formulahendry (Microsoft employee) maintained — good security practices. TypeScript project. No compliance certifications (local tool). Output files (specs/requirements.md, specs/design.md) written to project directory — review permissions. No sensitive data handling. Spec artifacts may contain proprietary design details — handle accordingly.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Node.js 20+ requirement. Community maintained by Microsoft employee. No formal breaking change policy. Idempotency: yes (spec files are deterministic given same input). Stage ordering requirement creates dependency failure mode. Output files overwritten without versioning — use version control for spec files. MCP prompt support required in client — not all MCP clients support prompts. No retry guidance.

af_score_components.notes: Structured task management MCP server for AI coding agents (quality 75). No external auth — DATA_DIR env var controls storage location. DATA_DIR must be set or task data is ephemeral. Task IDs are opaque — agents must list tasks before referencing by ID. PROMPT_LANGUAGE misconfiguration can cause template mismatches. Web GUI (ENABLE_GUI=true) exposes task data on network WITHOUT authentication. Partial idempotency. JSON-based persistent storage. React web UI for monitoring. Chain-of-thought reflection pattern before implementation. Good for multi-day AI coding projects needing persistent task state.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: ENABLE_GUI=true exposes task data on network without authentication — do not enable GUI in shared or multi-user environments. Local operation otherwise — no network exposure. DATA_DIR controls task data location — file permissions are the security boundary. No external credentials stored. Task data stored in plain JSON files — may contain proprietary project context. Community maintained TypeScript/React project. No compliance certifications. PROMPT_LANGUAGE env var can alter agent behavior if misconfigured.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Active community development. No formal breaking change policy. DATA_DIR must be set for persistence — ephemeral by default is a reliability risk. Task IDs require list-first pattern before operations. PROMPT_LANGUAGE misconfiguration causes template failures. No retry guidance. Partial idempotency. Web GUI unreliable for production (no auth). JSON storage not suited for concurrent multi-agent writes. Good for single-agent multi-session workflows with human supervision.

af_score_components.notes: Community MCP server for Microsoft Teams via Graph API (quality 65). Requires Azure Entra ID app registration with 6 env vars: TEAMS_APP_ID, TEAMS_APP_PASSWORD, TEAMS_APP_TYPE, TEAMS_APP_TENANT_ID, TEAM_ID, TEAMS_CHANNEL_ID. Client credentials (app) OAuth flow. TEAMS_CHANNEL_ID must be URL-encoded — raw IDs from Teams UI will not work. Single-tenant vs multi-tenant configuration must match Azure app registration exactly. No pagination documented for message history — large channels may be truncated. Creating duplicate threads on retry is a risk (no idempotency keys). Docker image available from GitHub Container Registry. InditexTech (Inditex/Zara) maintained.

security_score_components.security_notes: Microsoft Teams and Graph API enforce HTTPS. Microsoft 365 compliance boundaries apply. Azure Entra ID client credentials — enterprise-grade OAuth. Graph API permissions can be scoped to specific Teams operations during app registration. InditexTech (enterprise company) maintained — reasonable security practices. App credentials via 6 environment variables — manage as secrets. App registration with Graph API permissions is an IT security event — requires Azure admin approval. Channel message content may include sensitive business communications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Microsoft Teams has SLA and high uptime. Community MCP server has no formal SLA. No retry guidance. TEAMS_CHANNEL_ID URL-encoding is a reliability pitfall. No pagination for message history — large channels silently truncated. Thread creation not idempotent — retry creates duplicates. Single-tenant vs multi-tenant mismatch causes auth failures. Azure Entra ID client credentials require periodic refresh. No formal breaking change policy for community MCP server. Microsoft Graph API versioning provides stability for underlying APIs.
